text.title: Event Store Database Schema
text.summary: PostgreSQL DDL for the events table, snapshots table, and example projection tables.

example.note: UNIQUE (stream_id, version) is the database-level optimistic concurrency guard. event_id UNIQUE prevents duplicate event insertion on replay.
One snapshot per stream (UPSERT on stream_id). Stores the aggregate state at a given version to bound replay time.

example.code:
CREATE TABLE order_details ( order_id UUID PRIMARY KEY, customer_id UUID NOT NULL, status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, placed_at TIMESTAMP, shipped_at TIMESTAMP );
CREATE TABLE order_items ( id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY, order_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES order_details(order_id), product_id UUID NOT NULL, product_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, quantity INTEGER NOT NULL, unit_price DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL );

text.reference: Full DDL file ready to run
